metadata.notes: agent=yes; daemon=full (PID-liveness skip, provisional on cwd+cmdline bind); driver=gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (provisional on PID bind)
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts three user-observable signals: (1) the session does NOT disappear past `readyTTL`, (2) state remains `ready` during the idle window, and (3) the next user prompt resumes `ready → working` normally, with tool calls ≤ 0.

### agent = yes
Mistral Vibe (v2.19.0, `/Users/merlin/.local/bin/vibe`) is a long-lived interactive Python console-script REPL. Once launched it sits in its prompt loop until the user sends the next prompt or exits — it does not self-terminate at turn completion. Real transcripts under `~/.vibe/logs/session/` show multi-turn sessions whose terminal message is a text-only assistant reply. Long-idle-live is baseline REPL persistence, no special feature required.

### daemon = full (provisional)
The persistence mechanism is daemon-side and adapter-agnostic. `PIDManager.sweepStaleSnapshot` (core/application/services/pid_manager.go:942-948) first tests `isStaleUpdatedAt(updatedAt, readyTTL)` — during idle the JSONL stops appending, so `UpdatedAt` freezes and eventually crosses the TTL — then, before deleting, returns early when `snap.pid > 0 && syscall.Kill(pid,0) == nil`. So an idle-past-TTL session backed by a live PID is NOT swept. The vibe adapter wires exactly what this path needs: `Process.Match = CommandPattern{processCmdRegex}` + `PIDForSession = DiscoverPID`, which binds the running Python `vibe` process by cwd+command-line (pid.go / DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine), the cwd sourced from the meta.json sidecar. The turn arc is produced by parser.go: `role:user` → user_message (ready → working); a text-only `role:assistant` (no tool_calls) → turn_done (working → ready). Simple echo prompts drive zero tool calls, satisfying `tool calls ≤ 0`.

Why provisional (confidence 0.72, not 0.9): liveness hinges entirely on `DiscoverPID` actually binding `pid > 0`. Vibe is a console-script whose OS process name is the interpreter, so the match is by cmdline+cwd, not process name — a shape only a live drive can confirm fires. If the bind fails, `snap.pid == 0` and `reapUnboundReadyGhost` (pid_manager.go:938) sweeps the ready session once its transcript goes stale — the exact disappearance the scenario must exclude. Reading the parser proves emission of the state arc but not that PID discovery succeeds; `record` promotes this from provisional to settled.

### driver = gap:wait_turn
The recipe templates claudecode's: send → wait_turn → sleep 20 → send → wait_turn → sleep 5. The freshly-scaffolded driver `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` has its turn-done seam (SEAM 2) still stubbed — `wait_turn() { not_implemented wait_turn }` (lines 168-170) — and its `DRIVE_ELICITS` constant is `"send slash sleep"`, which omits `wait_turn`. `recipe-lint`'s semantic check (`recipe_semantic_gaps`) reads `DRIVE_ELICITS` and would flag `not-elicited:wait_turn` before recording. This contradicts the dispatch note's claim that `wait_turn` is real; verified against source, it is not. This is a tooling gap, not an observability limit: the cell keeps its real recipe + spec and `record` ports the turn-done poll (from drive-claudecode/codex-interactive.sh) before driving.

### observations
model = `mistral-medium-3.5` (confirmed from a live `~/.vibe/logs/session/*/meta.json` `config.active_model` on this machine; `NormalizeModelName` leaves it unchanged; mistral models are absent from core/pkg/capacity so the context-WINDOW is unresolved, but the model NAME is emitted every event via the sidecar). tokens_nonzero = true: turn_done emits `Tokens.Total` from `stats.context_tokens` (parser.go:112). cost_nonzero = false: the daemon does NOT emit per-turn cost — sidecar.go decodes only working_directory/active_model/context_tokens and never reads `session_cost`, and the parser never sets `Cost`.
caveats: daemon=full is provisional on PID binding: DiscoverPID must bind pid>0 for the Python vibe console-script via cwd+cmdline. If it fails, reapUnboundReadyGhost (pid_manager.go:938) sweeps the idle ready session — the exact failure the scenario excludes. record confirms the bind fires.
model 'mistral-medium-3.5' is the confirmed local default from a live meta.json; if the recording's active_model differs, record should correct the observation.
tokens_nonzero=true assumes vibe writes a non-zero stats.context_tokens to meta.json by the first turn_done; provisional until record confirms the sidecar is populated early.
Recording exercises a ~15-20s idle window; production sessions idle for hours. duration_at_least_ms is a floor, so longer real idles still pass.
context-WINDOW (max) is not resolved for mistral models (absent from core/pkg/capacity); only the raw context-token count is emitted. The scenario asserts neither, so daemon stays full.

sources.note: sweepStaleSnapshot (942-948): idle-past-readyTTL session is NOT deleted while snap.pid>0 && Kill(pid,0)==nil — the adapter-agnostic PID-liveness skip. reapUnboundReadyGhost (938) is the pid==0 failure path.
Process.Match = CommandPattern{processCmdRegex} + PIDForSession = DiscoverPID — the adapter wires the PID binding the liveness skip depends on.
user→user_message (working); text-only assistant (no tool_calls)→turn_done (ready). applySidecar emits ModelName every event and Tokens.Total from context_tokens on turn_done; never sets Cost.
Decodes only environment.working_directory, config.active_model, stats.context_tokens — session_cost is NOT read, so per-turn cost is never emitted (cost_nonzero:false).

SEAM 2 wait_turn() { not_implemented wait_turn } (168-170) and DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" — wait_turn is stubbed and not elicited, so recipe-lint flags not-elicited:wait_turn (driver=gap:wait_turn).
Live sidecar: config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, stats.context_tokens=136662, stats.session_cost present but unread by the adapter, parent_session_id=null.
Sibling template for this scenario: same 6-step recipe (send/wait_turn/sleep×2) and same PID-liveness rationale.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (2.19.0) and authenticated (MISTRAL_API_KEY or vibe config)
irrlichd running with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s in its environment (the daemon's default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording)
irrlichd in --record mode with the transcripts permission granted
record must PORT the wait_turn turn-done poll into driver-interactive.sh (SEAM 2) before driving — it is currently stubbed
setup: Driver launches vibe under tmux in a fresh real cwd; the session dir + messages.jsonl and meta.json sidecar materialize at launch. cwd is read from meta.json for PID binding.
The first post-turn sleep (20s) is ~1.33x the 15s TTL — long enough to prove the pid-liveness skip (pid_manager.go:942-948) holds the bound session past readyTTL without burning a full minute.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: the vibe session binds pid>0 (cwd resolved from meta.json, matched via processCmdRegex) after turn 1 enters ready
no transcript_removed for the session during the 20s idle window (not swept while the vibe process is alive)
no state_transition to working or waiting during the idle window (state stays ready)
after the second send: ready → working → ready for the SAME session
final session state is ready; tool calls = 0
